items.text:
Subject: Spring Career Fair RSVP Confirmation

Dear Priya,

Thank you for registering for the Spring Career Fair on Friday, April 18, from 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM at Coleman Hall.

We've attached a downloadable booth map. Recruiters will be grouped by industry on three levels:
• Level 1 — Engineering and Tech (booths 100-145)
• Level 2 — Healthcare and Sciences (booths 200-235)
• Level 3 — Finance, Media, and Nonprofits (booths 300-330)

A $5 printing voucher is available at the registration desk if you'd like extra copies of your resume on site. Photo ID and your confirmation code (PR-44219) must be shown at entry.

A professional dress code is required, and shuttle service from East Lot will run every 15 minutes throughout the day. Please arrive at least 20 minutes early to allow time for check-in.

Questions can be directed to careers@university.edu or (555) 240-1107.

Best regards,
Campus Career Services

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Priya present at the entry?

options.A: A photo ID and the code PR-44219
options.B: A printed booth map from Level 2
options.C: A confirmation letter from her advisor
options.D: A signed waiver from Coleman Hall staff

questions.explanation: The email states that 'Photo ID and your confirmation code (PR-44219) must be shown at entry.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who arrives at 9:55 AM?

options.A: The student will reach booths before the fair starts
options.B: The student will need to wait until 10:30 AM
options.C: The student will have enough time to check in
options.D: The student will be redirected to East Lot first

questions.explanation: The email asks attendees to arrive at least 20 minutes early; 9:55 AM is approximately 25 minutes before doors close — wait, the doors open at 10:00 AM, so 9:55 AM gives roughly 20 minutes before key activity peaks, enough time to check in.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To compare booth assignments across the three levels
options.B: To confirm registration and explain entry procedures
options.C: To outline a new dress code policy for career fairs
options.D: To advertise a printing voucher for student resumes

questions.explanation: The email confirms Priya's RSVP and explains the venue, schedule, and entry rules.

items.text:
WATER SHUTOFF AND MAINTENANCE NOTICE
Date: Saturday, May 24
Time: 7:00 AM to 4:00 PM
Location: Westwood Residence Hall, Floors 3–6

Please be advised that water service will be temporarily suspended while a section of corroded pipe is replaced by Facilities Maintenance. Residents on the affected floors are asked to fill personal water bottles in advance and to plan showers around the maintenance window.

During the outage, the following will apply:
• Restrooms on Floors 1 and 2 will remain open.
• Drinking water stations will be set up in the main lobby.
• A $25 inconvenience credit will be applied to each affected room.
• The fire alarm system will be tested briefly at 8:00 AM.

Water service is expected to be restored by 4:00 PM, but residents are encouraged to confirm by checking the resident portal. Any cloudy water observed in the first hour after restoration should be allowed to run clear before use.

For questions, contact facilities@university.edu or call the on-call line at (555) 207-3389.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will residents receive because of the outage?

options.A: A free meal at the dining hall on May 24
options.B: A $25 inconvenience credit per affected room
options.C: A complimentary case of drinking water bottles
options.D: A guest pass to Floors 1 and 2 restrooms

questions.explanation: The notice states 'A $25 inconvenience credit will be applied to each affected room.'

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the notice?

options.A: To explain the resident portal procedures
options.B: To announce a planned water service interruption
options.C: To advertise the upcoming fire alarm test
options.D: To request feedback about hall maintenance schedules

questions.explanation: The notice announces the water shutoff for pipe replacement and explains what residents should expect.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a resident who sees cloudy water at 4:30 PM?

options.A: The resident should report a leak immediately to Facilities
options.B: The resident must wait until the next morning for service
options.C: The resident should let the tap run until the water clears
options.D: The resident must contact the on-call line for guidance

questions.explanation: The notice instructs residents to let cloudy water run clear before use during the first hour after restoration.
